title: carmichaelia exsul
intro: Carmichaelia exsul, also known as the outlying broom, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. Traditionally used in Maori medicine, this plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat various health issues such as colds, fever, and skin problems. The health benefits of Carmichaelia exsul include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
